{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query is whether FKBP52 encodes a chaperone. In the MEDCORP source documents, several references mention FKBP proteins in the context of chaperone function. Document [23] explicitly states: 'A defect in collagen chaperones that accompany procollagen molecules from ER to Golgi apparatus 2. Autosomal recessive [28] [29] Specific defects: SERPINH1 - type X, FKBP10 - type XI.' This indicates that FKBP10 is a collagen chaperone. While the document refers to FKBP10, it is part of a family of FKBP proteins known to function as chaperones. Additionally, in Document [27], it is noted that calcineurin inhibitors bind to immunophilins, including FK-binding proteins (FKBPs), which are chaperone proteins. This establishes that FKBP proteins, in general, function as chaperones. Although the specific mention of FKBP52 is not directly stated, FKBP52 is a known member of the FKBP family, and the family is well-documented as chaperone proteins. The naming convention (FKBP) and the functional context in the literature strongly support that FKBP52 is a chaperone. Therefore, based on the established biological role of the FKBP family, FKBP52 encodes a chaperone.", "answer_choice": "A"}
